protocol.name: aether_ipc_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: aether_ipc_manager_v1
interface.description:
This global interface is the entry point for all IPC communication with the Aether Wayland compositor. Clients bind it via wl_registry and immediately start receiving workspace and keyboard state events.
The compositor sends the initial state as soon as the bind completes so clients can initialise without an explicit query.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the aether_ipc_manager_v1 object. The compositor will stop sending events to this client.

request.name: set_workspace
request.description:
Request that the compositor change the active workspace on the output identified by output_id to the workspace at grid position (x, y).
Grid coordinates are zero-based. The compositor will reply with a workspace_state event reflecting the new state.

event.name: workspace_state
event.description:
Sent whenever the active workspace on any output changes, and once immediately after the client binds the interface.
grid_width × grid_height gives the total number of workspaces. x and y are the coordinates of the currently active workspace (zero-based, within the grid).

event.name: keyboard_state
event.description:
Sent whenever the active keyboard layout changes, and once immediately after the client binds the interface.
layouts is a newline-separated list of layout names (e.g. "English\nArabic"). layout_index is the zero-based index of the currently active layout within that list.
